user_query: Build me an ADHD-friendly daily plan for today using the 3x time-estimation rule and the 3 Things system. Read these files for my context and constraints: - input/user_context.json (tasks with my naive time estimates, energy windows, preferences, flags for crisis/hyperfocus) - input/calendar.csv (today’s fixed commitments, in 24-hour HH:MM format) - input/dopamine_menu_seed.md (a rough list I want tightened up). Deliver all outputs under output/ as follows: 1) output/plan.json — Include: - date - the_thing (string), would_be_nice (array), if_crushing_it (array) - task_estimates (for every task from user_context.json: naive_estimate_minutes and adjusted_estimate_minutes using the 3x rule) - time_blocks (array of objects with: start_time, end_time, type in {meeting, task, buffer, break}, label). Ensure every meeting from input/calendar.csv appears with exact times and that a 10–15 minute buffer directly follows each meeting. Use 24-hour times and include at least two named transition buffers. - executive_support (at least two items, e.g., "2-minute start", "body doubling", "temptation bundling") 2) output/agenda.md — A human-readable schedule for today that: - Lists THE Thing, Would Be Nice, and If I’m On Fire clearly - Shows a time-blocked agenda in 24-hour HH:MM with buffers visible - Includes a mini Dopamine Menu with sections: Appetizers, Starters, Main Courses, Desserts - Ends with a Shutdown Ritual of 5 numbered steps 3) output/dopamine_menu_edited.md — A concise, edited version of input/dopamine_menu_seed.md that: - Uses the four headings: Appetizers, Starters, Main Courses, Desserts - Has no more than 15 bullet items total across all sections - Avoids filler words: "very", "really", "just" - Is under 2,000 characters total Keep the tone supportive (no guilt/shame). Use my morning high-energy window for THE Thing, protect it from meetings when possible, and include transition buffers around hard switches. Make all file paths relative and do not include any absolute or environment-specific paths.
